title: Rhythm: punctuation flow
description: Rhythm exercise mixing all punctuation in natural sentences
finger_focus: All learned chars - punctuation in flow

tips.text: No new keys. We hold what you know and tidy up the rhythm, the way you would tidy a sentence before it goes to print.
Keep your hands on home position and your gaze ahead. Repeat each word evenly, the same pace from first letter to last. Don't speed up near the end.

tips.title: A regular pulse
tips.text: Set a quiet beat in your head and keep to it. Equal pauses, no sprinting, no stopping. Even keystrokes read like clean copy.

tips.title: Rhythm: today
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: today today today. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: great
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: great great great. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: laughed
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: laughed laughed laughed. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.text: When every keystroke lands on time, your hands stop thinking and simply work. That calm, regular pulse is what carries you through a long page without tiring.
Worked through every step? Open the trainer below and make one full run on the lesson text. That run saves your progress and unlocks the next lesson.

character_tips.anna: Punctuation in flow - practice until commas don't break your rhythm.
character_tips.maxim: Pro typists treat punctuation as just more characters. Same speed.
character_tips.klavochka: Like a song with pauses, sunshine - beautiful and natural! 🎵
